title: Platanthera helleborina
taxonomy: Platanthera helleborina is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and the genus Platanthera. The genus Platanthera is part of the subtribe Orchideae, tribe Orchideae, subfamily Orchidoideae, and family Orchidaceae. This classification indicates the evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics between Platanthera helleborina and other orchids. The classification of Platanthera helleborina can be further refined with more specific botanical classifications.
uses: Platanthera helleborina, also known as the fragrant orchid, has been traditionally used in some folk remedies. intro: Platanthera helleborina, also known as the fragrant orchid, is a perennial plant species belonging to the orchid family. Traditionally, Platanthera helleborina has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
